observation: The repo root does not ship a `package.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, or `openclaw.plugin.json`; package-specific commands run inside package directories.

`openclaw node` remains a product-level host service rather than a publishable package from this repo, and repo-local host-lane boundary notes now live directly in `docs/OPENCLAW_NODE_INSTALL_AND_IDENTITY_CONTRACT.md` plus the active package docs.
Repo-local planning scratch files belong only under ignored `.local-planning/`, while `.codex/` and `memory.jsonl` remain local workflow artifacts rather than shipped package surfaces.
As of 2026-04-17, tracked repo docs, local agent/sub-agent docs, package docs, MCP memory, and the local `memory.jsonl` snapshot are synchronized to the closed post-migration canon.
As of 2026-04-17, the active tracked canon for plugin packages is documented in `docs/PLUGIN_PACKAGE_CANON.md` and `docs/PLUGIN_STYLE_CANON.md`.

The active local package canon uses `src/runtime/` for production code, flat default test files in `src/test/`, and shared `src/test/fixtures/`, `src/test/helpers/`, and optional `src/test/mocks/` with README-driven `reuse first` policy.

The active local style canon requires domain-named runtime extraction instead of generic dump files, action-oriented naming for exported functions, strict TypeScript with no `any` by default, typed result models for expected runtime outcomes, and positive plus negative contract-path test coverage.

Host execution is node-bound and runs through `node.invoke` with `system.run.prepare` followed by `system.run`; the bundled skill surface is intentionally collapsed to the single primary user-facing entrypoint `send_to_git` / `отправь в гит`.
Verification baseline for the active plugin package is `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test`, and `pnpm pack:smoke`.
In this repository, `отправь в гит` means the full flow: separate branch per change intent, commit, push, PR to `main`, checks polling, merge, and local `main` sync.

Default grouping rule is one branch and one PR per single change intent, with package runtime, repo docs, companion docs, memory sync, and `.codex` governance split by ownership unless the merged state would otherwise be misleading.

2026-04-17: In this repository, for `отправь в гит` flow do not auto-delete the branch during PR merge unless the user explicitly requests branch deletion; merge and local `main` sync are the required default steps, branch cleanup is not.

Sub-agent timing canon is pre-PR by default per completed change intent: run the owning implementation lane first, then only the matching auditors or doc-sync roles; when a canon is adopted after a live package already exists, run one catch-up audit before returning to normal per-slice checks; `memory-sync` mirrors the accepted state only after owner files are updated.

After a successful git or PR flow, completion messages must not append unsolicited caveats about excluded files or ignored local artifacts unless that omission blocks the requested outcome or the user explicitly asks about scope.
For OpenClaw research in this workspace, source priority is `https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw` first, `https://github.com/openclaw` second, and Context7 only after those official GitHub sources.
Do not suggest or perform git flow for local-only `.codex/` workflow updates unless the user explicitly overrides that boundary.
Codex project-scoped config can live in `.codex/config.toml`; MCP and local config behavior should be interpreted from the live repo plus the active trusted-project context.

In this repository, a request to `sync memory` or `синхронизировать память` means both MCP memory and the local `memory.jsonl` snapshot unless the user explicitly narrows scope; memory sync is not complete until both are updated.
Memory synchronization in this repository includes removing stale wording, outdated phrasing, and superseded canon language from both MCP memory and the local `memory.jsonl` snapshot.

Docs sync on 2026-04-17 aligned `docs/CLAWHUB_PUBLISH_PREFLIGHT.md` to official ClawHub publish syntax (`clawhub package publish <source>` and `clawhub skill publish <path>`), aligned `docs/OPENCLAW_NODE_INSTALL_AND_IDENTITY_CONTRACT.md` to official node install/pairing commands, and synced repo canon docs plus root README to the active domain-grouped runtime layout.

When concrete drift is discovered in a live package surface, commands, install/publish flow, node boundary, canon wording, or sync workflow, the repository now treats it as a scoped catch-up audit trigger for the affected ownership cluster rather than as a blanket repo-wide recheck.
The default scoped catch-up audit cluster is `package-auditor`, `structure-auditor`, `repo-docs-sync` and/or `openclaw-docs-sync`, `style-governor`, and `memory-sync` as the final mirror step.

`openclaw-host-git-workflow/api.ts` no longer depends on the non-canonical compile-time `@openclaw/plugin-sdk` type import; the package now keeps local tool/api typing while continuing to use the official `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` runtime subpath contract.

2026-04-17: `docs/OPENCLAW_NODE_INSTALL_AND_IDENTITY_CONTRACT.md` now states that repo-local docs prefer `openclaw devices list|approve|reject` for gateway-side pairing approval, while `openclaw nodes pending|approve|reject` remains an alternate official CLI surface rather than a separate repo-owned node package.

Doc sync for touched repo or package surfaces should be treated as a pre-PR gate by default; if docs are intentionally deferred into a post-merge follow-up, the repository now requires one short drift check immediately after `PR merged` plus local `main` sync, limited to the touched surfaces rather than a blanket repo-wide audit.

This repository now separates repo-local role briefs under `.codex/subagents/*.md` from executable Codex custom-agent configs under `.codex/agents/*.toml`; the `.toml` layer carries display `nickname_candidates` in the `Agent X` format aligned to each repo-local lane or auditor role.

2026-04-17: The package now exposes a bounded `enter_branch` action that can start from `main` or another clean local branch, validates a non-main branch name, creates or switches to the requested local working branch, and keeps `push_branch`, `create_pr`, `wait_for_checks`, and `merge_pr` restricted to non-main branch execution.

2026-04-17: `enter_branch` now permits one bounded dirty-worktree path: when the current branch is `main` and the requested branch does not yet exist locally, it creates the new branch and carries uncommitted changes into it; dirty switches into existing branches remain blocked.

2026-04-17: Branch scopes, commit scopes, and merge-visible PR titles in this repository should identify the owning package slug or an explicit repo surface such as `repo`, `repo-docs`, or `.codex`, rather than generic labels like `workflow`.

2026-04-17: Local Codex sub-agent canon now applies the same package-aware naming rule in `.codex/subagents/README.md`, `git-lane`, `pr-lane`, and the executable `git-lane`/`pr-lane` agent configs, so branch prep and PR flow both treat package or repo-surface identification as mandatory naming context.

2026-04-17: The package now includes `src/test/host-git-workflow-tool.test.ts` to verify the public `host_git_workflow_action` dispatch layer for branch-aware planning output and confirmed-plan validation wording, in addition to lower-level runtime tests.

2026-04-17: `host-git-lane/` was removed after its surviving boundary and repo-resolution guidance was redistributed into `docs/OPENCLAW_NODE_INSTALL_AND_IDENTITY_CONTRACT.md`, the root `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and the active package docs.

2026-04-17: `OPENCLAW_MAC_HOST_NODE_BOOTSTRAP_TODO_RU_2026-04-17.md` now points to the current owner docs surfaces `docs/OPENCLAW_NODE_INSTALL_AND_IDENTITY_CONTRACT.md` and `openclaw-host-git-workflow/README.md` instead of a separate companion-doc folder.

2026-04-17: Documentation now reflects the tested Docker/macOS path across the root README, `openclaw-host-git-workflow/README.md`, and `docs/OPENCLAW_NODE_INSTALL_AND_IDENTITY_CONTRACT.md`, including gateway token versus operator pairing, exact gateway-side approval by `requestId`, and `nodeSelector` guidance for `openclaw-docker-host-git`.

2026-04-17: The bounded workflow was tested through branch entry, commit, host-side push, PR creation, checks wait, and merge; bounded local `main` sync correctly stopped on a dirty worktree, and the remaining repo-doc versus package-doc follow-up was split into separate PRs (#81 repo docs, #82 package docs) before local `main` was fully synced again.

2026-04-17: The active package docs now document the tested same-machine Docker gateway plus macOS host-node setup, including the need for separate CLI/operator pairing before host-node use and explicit `config.nodeSelector: "openclaw-docker-host-git"` for the plugin entry when binding the bounded workflow.

2026-04-17: For the tested same-machine path, package verification passed (`pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm pack:smoke`), the plugin loaded from the local path install, and the bounded workflow completed branch/PR/merge flow end to end with `sync_main` correctly blocked on a dirty worktree.

2026-04-17: The repository now has dedicated local governance roles for structure and style under `.codex/subagents/structure-auditor.md` and `.codex/subagents/style-governor.md`, plus matching executable agent configs under `.codex/agents/`.

2026-04-17: The tested macOS hardening note now sets `browser.enabled = false`, `gateway.nodes.browser.mode = "off"`, and `nodeHost.browserProxy.enabled = false` before restarting the node host; official docs confirm these settings reduce browser-related surface and browser proxy exposure, but the local test still showed `browser` in node caps, so the accepted repo wording treats this as surface reduction rather than proof that browser claims disappear.

2026-04-18: `main` now includes merged PR `#83` (`docs/openclaw-host-git-workflow-live-cycle-validation`), which closed a live cycle validation slice with five runtime fixes: host-side repo-root preflight, multiline PR body handling, latest commit metadata without `git --pretty=%...`, per-field `gh pr view --json <field>` reads instead of multi-field argv, and bounded success when a PR has no required checks.
2026-04-19: Prefers local agent release flow in openclaw-automation-tools to publish GitHub Releases by default and to leave ClawHub publication as a manual, explicit-request operator step.

2026-04-19: Local release governance now treats GitHub Release publication as the default public-release closure; ClawHub publish/manual upload remains optional and package README plus tracked release notes must stay usable as manual operator guidance.

2026-04-19: When ClawHub publication is explicitly requested, release prep must also generate a companion worksheet file at docs/releases/<package-slug>/vX.Y.Z.clawhub.md with fill-in fields for source repo, source commit, source ref, source path, publish flow, owner/publisher, and operator notes.
2026-04-19: For explicit ClawHub publication in openclaw-automation-tools, wants agents to generate a dedicated fill-in worksheet file for the UI instead of reconstructing source metadata ad hoc.

2026-04-19: The tracked docs/releases/<package-slug>/vX.Y.Z.md file is the canonical GitHub Release note source and tracked release record; when ClawHub publication is explicitly requested, the companion docs/releases/<package-slug>/vX.Y.Z.clawhub.md worksheet owns the manual ClawHub UI fields.

2026-04-19: release-lane now owns companion docs/releases/<package-slug>/vX.Y.Z.clawhub.md worksheet generation for explicit ClawHub publication requests, in addition to GitHub-first release-note drafting.

2026-04-19: The release-governance sync covered AGENTS.md, local sub-agent briefs, docs/RELEASES.md, docs/CLAWHUB_PUBLISH_PREFLIGHT.md, and the openclaw-session-bloat-warning release templates and ledger files.

2026-04-19: The default public release closes when the package version is aligned, the merge is on main, the package-qualified tag is pushed, the GitHub Release is published from that tag, verification is green, and the matching docs/releases/<package-slug>/vX.Y.Z.md record is stored.

2026-04-18: Local verification in this workspace after syncing the stale host-ops test expectations shows `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build`, and `pnpm test` pass for `openclaw-host-git-workflow`; the reported missing native `rolldown` binding appears environment-specific and did not reproduce here.

2026-04-17: The local operator runbook, checklist, copy-paste session script, and Mac host-node bootstrap TODO for the tested same-machine Docker/macOS path were all closed and archived under `.local-planning/archive/` after owner docs, governance, MCP memory, and local `memory.jsonl` were synced.

2026-04-17: The accepted package surface now includes setup doctor, repo-aware planning, branch-aware planning, explicit `commit_prep`, repo resolution, live host node binding, host preflight, bounded `enter_branch`, confirmed-plan validation, bounded push, bounded PR creation, required-check waiting, bounded merge, and bounded local `main` sync.

2026-04-17: `commit_prep` now materializes the current-state matrix, tested bounded flow, ownership grouping, branch naming, commit-body suggestions, and recommended short-session choreography inside the package.

2026-04-17: The active package docs and bundled skill now describe the preferred short-session flow `doctor -> plan_with_branches -> commit_prep -> execution kernel`; docs sync stays a separate follow-up only when shipped truth changed, and memory sync is not part of the plugin model.
2026-04-17: Package metadata, plugin manifest, runtime descriptions, planning metadata text, and tests now align on the active shipped surface that includes setup doctor and explicit commit prep.

2026-04-17: Repo docs sync now reflects the active package surface as setup doctor, repo-aware planning, branch-aware planning, explicit commit prep, bounded branch entry, confirmed-plan validation, bounded push, bounded PR creation, bounded wait for required checks, bounded merge, bounded local `main` sync, and the plugin verification order `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm pack:smoke`.

2026-04-17: The productization slice is framed as product-facing `doctor -> plan_with_branches -> commit_prep -> execution kernel`; memory sync remains only a repo-maintenance concern and is not part of the OpenClaw-facing plugin flow.

2026-04-17: Repo governance wording now enumerates the active `openclaw-host-git-workflow` surface as setup doctor, repo-aware planning, branch-aware planning, explicit commit prep, bounded branch entry, confirmed-plan validation, bounded push, bounded PR creation, bounded wait for checks, bounded merge, and bounded local `main` sync.

2026-04-18: Repo-local agent orchestration now uses an explicit `Idea Gate` on the top-level lane before non-trivial planning, evidence-backed plan blocks with `What`, `Why`, `Evidence`, `Checklist`, and `Done when`, and a dependent-slice rule that waits for verification, PR merge, and local `main` sync before the next slice begins.

2026-04-18: The local planning source of truth now defaults to one Markdown task file under `.local-planning/<task>.md` with GitHub-style checkboxes; `.local-planning/TEMPLATE_LOCAL_TASK_PLAN_RU.md` is the accepted template for new repo-local task artifacts.

2026-04-18: The orchestration planning artifact `REPO_AGENT_ORCHESTRATION_PLAN_RU_2026-04-17.md` was closed after governance sync, template adoption, wording audit, and memory sync, then moved into `.local-planning/archive/` in the same completion slice.

2026-04-18: Repo-local planning now has two accepted scales: ordinary work stays in one Markdown task file, while larger accepted work uses one master plan plus linked child checklist files under `.local-planning/`; the master plan remains the source of truth for the whole rollout.

2026-04-18: The top-level `.local-planning/` directory now carries the accepted local planning templates, while closed repo-local planning artifacts live under `.local-planning/archive/` after outcome sync and memory sync complete.

2026-04-18: For local host-shell commands in this repository, avoid double-quoted shell strings that contain Markdown backticks or other shell-significant body text; prefer argv-safe execution, single-quoted heredocs, or file-based bodies. 2026-04-18: In this repository, a plain `sync` / `синхронизируй` request means reconcile the named surfaces with each other and current live canon, not implicit tracked-git publication; ignored local-only surfaces such as `AGENTS.md`, `.codex/**`, `.local-planning/**`, and `memory.jsonl` stay local-only unless the user explicitly asks to track or send them to git.

2026-04-18: `openclaw-canon/` is the active operational-canon plugin package with typed tools `canon_status`, `canon_doctor`, and preview-first `canon_fix`, bundled `canon-memory-hygiene` and `canon-source-of-truth-fix` skills, file-backed state for summaries and preview tokens, and passing local verification via `pnpm check`.

2026-04-18: `AGENTS.md` is now the concise canonical entrypoint for shared repo-local agent rules, while detailed shared mechanics live under `.codex/governance/orchestration.md` and `.codex/governance/commands-and-git.md`.

2026-04-18: `.codex/subagents/README.md` now acts as an index only, `.codex/subagents/*.md` are role-local briefs, and `.codex/agents/*.toml` are thin executable wrappers that point back to the role briefs and shared governance files.
2026-04-18: The top-level agent is now the default planner, coordinator, and final integrator; bounded local execution is allowed only for work that is not delegated.

2026-04-18: Local Codex sub-agents are the default execution path for bounded work with clear ownership; keep work local only when the task is too small, too tightly coupled, urgent on the immediate critical path, explicitly non-delegable by user request, or blocked by overlapping write scopes.
2026-04-18: Completed or no-longer-needed sub-agents should be closed promptly, and stale idle agents should be closed before spawning more work, to avoid local agent thread-limit failures.

2026-04-18: After substantial accepted changes to repo governance, agent topology, plugin surfaces, package docs, or repo docs, the repo now requires syncing both MCP memory and the local `memory.jsonl` snapshot before the slice is fully closed; `.local-planning/**`-only changes do not trigger that requirement on their own.

2026-04-18: Default MCP behavior now lives in `.codex/governance/orchestration.md`: use sequential thinking for non-trivial design/planning work, use Context7 for current library/framework behavior when relevant, and use memory only for durable accepted facts rather than transient planning state.

2026-04-18: Local governance now requires a memory sync after substantial accepted changes to agents, plugins, package docs, repo docs, or other canon-bearing surfaces, with both MCP memory and `memory.jsonl` updated together.

2026-04-18: For this repo, do not leave completed sub-agents open by default; close them after use and close stale idle agents before spawning new lanes, because the local environment enforces a bounded concurrent agent thread limit.

2026-04-18: `docs/OPENCLAW_NODE_INSTALL_AND_IDENTITY_CONTRACT.md` now states explicitly that the gateway and node host are product-level OpenClaw surfaces and are not shipped from this repo.

2026-04-18: Root `README.md` now reflects all four active repo canon/operational docs together: `docs/PLUGIN_PACKAGE_CANON.md`, `docs/PLUGIN_STYLE_CANON.md`, `docs/OPENCLAW_NODE_INSTALL_AND_IDENTITY_CONTRACT.md`, and `docs/CLAWHUB_PUBLISH_PREFLIGHT.md`.

2026-04-18: Root `README.md` now documents the planner package's concrete published entry surfaces as bundled skills `openclaw-workflow-planner`, `openclaw-workflow-research`, `openclaw-workflow-implementer`, plus the typed tool `workflow_planner_action`.
2026-04-18: Root README verification wording now points to `docs/CLAWHUB_PUBLISH_PREFLIGHT.md` for the publish-grade manual pre-publish gate beyond the CI/package-script minimum.

2026-04-18: In this repository, `fork_context` is a best-effort convenience for custom roles rather than required orchestration infrastructure; if a custom-role spawn rejects or does not support `fork_context`, retry once without fork and pass explicit task goal, ownership boundary, relevant paths, governing canon files, and known blocking findings in the handoff.

2026-04-18: Before any catch-up or audit-heavy slice, reduce the scope into explicit ownership clusters such as `openclaw-canon`, `openclaw-workflow-planner`, `openclaw-host-git-workflow`, `openclaw-session-bloat-warning`, or separate repo-owned surfaces; do not carry one broad multi-package write set into implementation.

2026-04-18: Before spawning a new auditor batch, close completed read-only lanes from the previous batch and confirm the next batch is still needed for the current ownership cluster; do not answer local agent thread-limit failures by opening more waits or broad repo-wide audit waves.

2026-04-18: For this repo, multi-package audit drift must be converted into separate ownership-cluster slices before edits or git grouping begin; when the write set or audit prompt is broad, split first and only then spawn bounded auditor or implementation lanes.

2026-04-18: After rebuilding `openclaw-host-git-workflow`, container-local `openclaw plugins install -l /home/node/tools/openclaw-host-git-workflow` succeeds without `--dangerously-force-unsafe-install`; the earlier false positive was eliminated by removing explicit `env: process.env` from the host ops runner.

2026-04-19: `release-lane` is the local Codex sub-agent role that owns bounded package release prep, publish preflight, manual ClawHub release-note artifacts, and durable release-record updates for one package at a time in `openclaw-automation-tools`.

2026-04-19: `github-release-lane` is the local Codex sub-agent role that owns package-qualified tag creation, pushed-tag verification, GitHub Release publication, and GitHub Release verification for one package release at a time in `openclaw-automation-tools`.

2026-04-20: The active package runtime is split into `src/runtime/planning/` and `src/runtime/state/` domains inside `openclaw-automation-tools`.
2026-04-20: Package verification passes `pnpm check`, covering lint, typecheck, build, test, and pack smoke for `openclaw-workflow-planner`.

2026-04-20: Shipped docs now state that `WORKFLOW_PLAN.md` is the single persisted planner file, control-plane metadata is embedded in or inferred from that state, and `idea_close` is part of the shipped workflow action flow.
2026-04-20: `controlPlane` is treated as a derived read model rebuilt from persisted `ideas` on load and save rather than a canonical persisted source inside `WORKFLOW_PLAN.md`.

2026-04-20: The planner no longer synthesizes persisted plan, task, or execution-brief artifact records for files that do not exist; `artifactRegistry` stays empty for the single-file planner surface.
2026-04-20: The workflow control plane no longer synthesizes `PlannerDesign` or `currentDesignId`, and `implementation_brief` remains a derived response without persisted brief metadata.
2026-04-20: Request phase semantics stay at `planning` after `plan_create`; generated checklist tasks alone do not advance the control plane to a brief-specific phase.

2026-04-20: If `idea_create` materially changes `problem`, `requestedOutcome`, or `ownerSurface` after downstream research, gate, plan, or task work already exists, the planner resets downstream lifecycle state back to `draft` so persisted state stays coherent.

2026-04-20: Planner checklist reconciliation during `task_done` and `plan_refresh` now follows stable task ids rather than matching task text; `task_done` prefers `taskId` and keeps 1-based `taskIndex` only as a legacy fallback.

2026-04-20: Shipped package docs and bundled skills now describe the gated planner flow explicitly: `idea_create` -> `research_attach` -> `idea_gate`, with `accepted`, `needs_research`, `deferred`, and `rejected` outcomes, and `plan_refresh` only after an accepted idea already has a plan.

2026-04-20: The `research_attach` contract is documented as typed evidence persisted before `idea_gate`, and the release README mirrors the skill-action matrix plus the `task_done` `taskId`-first contract.

2026-04-20: The repo now ships five live publishable plugin-plus-skill packages in lockstep across `docs/PLUGIN_PACKAGE_CANON.md`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, root `README.md`, `docs/CLAWHUB_PUBLISH_PREFLIGHT.md`, `docs/OPENCLAW_NODE_INSTALL_AND_IDENTITY_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/PLUGIN_STYLE_CANON.md`, `docs/RELEASES.md`, and `AGENTS.md`: `openclaw-host-git-workflow/`, `openclaw-workflow-planner/`, `openclaw-canon/`, `openclaw-session-bloat-warning/`, and `openclaw-url-tailwind-scaffold/`.

2026-04-20: `openclaw-url-tailwind-scaffold` is a live publishable plugin-plus-skill package in `openclaw-automation-tools`; its shipped surface is one primary tool entrypoint `url_tailwind_scaffold_action`, one bundled skill `openclaw-url-tailwind-scaffold`, no plugin config, no extra hooks, and one narrow working action `analyze_reference_page`.

2026-04-20: `url_tailwind_scaffold_action` returns acquisition metadata, a normalized shell contract for `app-shell`, `sidebar`, `header`, `content`, and `footer`, explicit synthetic `sourceBacked` versus `inferred` status fields derived from request mode, and a Tailwind CSS v4 scaffold summary with a generated file tree suggestion.

2026-04-20: V1 of `openclaw-url-tailwind-scaffold` is intentionally bounded to reference-URL-driven scaffold synthesis and does not yet claim live fetch-backed, browser-assisted, or auth-gated page analysis, pixel-perfect cloning, donor CSS-class reuse, file editing, or browser automation.
2026-04-20: The working slice accepts either a structured tool call or the bundled skill's raw command-dispatch form; raw input may be a plain URL or a compact JSON payload.

2026-04-20: The shipped runtime is grouped under `src/runtime/analysis/` and `src/runtime/contract/`, repo-level install and publish docs now treat the package as live, and package verification passed `pnpm check` in this slice.

2026-04-20: Host preflight now treats GitHub's authenticated `ssh -T git@github.com` response as SSH-ready even when the probe exits nonzero and returns the usual 'does not provide shell access' text.

2026-04-20: `wait_for_checks` now classifies required-check state from structured `gh pr checks --required --json ...` snapshots instead of relying on English substring matching for the no-checks case.

2026-04-20: The bundled `openclaw-host-git-workflow` skill was resynced to the shipped runtime surface and no longer describes `send_to_git`, host path mapping, confirmed-plan execution bridging, or structured result output as future work.
2026-04-20: Local package verification passed `pnpm check` again for `openclaw-host-git-workflow` after the SSH-readiness and required-checks fixes.
2026-04-20: `enableEarlyWarning` now disables both `llm_input` and `llm_output` signal tracking/state writes instead of only gating visible delivery.
2026-04-20: Package docs, manifest wording, and bundled skill now consistently describe the shipped runtime-risk heuristics as timeout-risk, lane-pressure, and no-reply signal reuse.
2026-04-20: Added regression coverage for disabled early-warning tracking and `no_reply_streak` classification/message behavior, and local package verification passed `pnpm check`.

2026-04-20: The shipped planner action surface includes `task_remove` and `task_reopen` alongside `task_add`, `task_done`, and `implementation_brief`; repo-level canon/docs were synced to that live surface.
2026-04-20: `plan_refresh` now preserves only unmatched manual extra tasks and no longer carries forward stale unmatched generated tasks.
2026-04-20: The planner loader now rejects explicit unsupported state versions instead of silently hydrating them as compatible.
2026-04-20: `idea_create` now preserves existing `links` when the update omits the `links` field, and local package verification passed `pnpm check`.

2026-04-20: Repo-owned docs now treat package `files` allowlists as the live packaging baseline for publishable plugin packages in this repository; package-local `.npmignore` is optional rather than a required canon element.

2026-04-20: Repo-level canon wording for `openclaw-session-bloat-warning` now includes timeout-risk, lane-pressure, and no-reply runtime-risk signal reuse, and repo-level planner wording now includes `task_remove` and `task_reopen`.

2026-04-21: Lane 1 design phase is now live in `openclaw-workflow-planner`: the tool surface adds `design_prepare` and `design_get`, `idea_gate` now points accepted work to `design_prepare`, and `plan_create` and `plan_refresh` require persisted design state first.

2026-04-21: Planner state moved to version 3 with persisted `PlannerDesign`, rebuilt `currentDesignId` control-plane pointers, markdown-rendered design sections in `WORKFLOW_PLAN.md`, and implementation briefs now derive from persisted design plus plan state.
2026-04-21: Package docs and runtime descriptions were synced to the lane-1 flow, and local verification passed `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm test` in `openclaw-workflow-planner`.

2026-04-21: Repo-level docs now describe `openclaw-workflow-planner` as a file-backed planner package with lane-1 design preparation and retrieval in addition to idea, research, plan, task, and implementation-brief surfaces.

2026-04-21: A container-spec-driven substrate slice landed in `openclaw-workflow-planner`: planner state moved to version 4, `WORKFLOW_PLAN.md` now serializes canonical planner state without derived `controlPlane`, plans gained durable `ideaId` and `currentSliceId`, task sets became persisted records with revisions, and canonical current-pointer structures are now hydrated from legacy state for design, plan, and task-set continuity.

2026-04-21: Legacy planner files from versions 1 through 3 now hydrate into the version-4 substrate while runtime control-plane views remain rebuild-derived; local verification passed `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm test`, and `pnpm build` in `openclaw-workflow-planner`.

2026-04-21: Work on `openclaw-workflow-planner` is now being driven strictly from the container canon in `openclaw-gateway:/home/node/workspace/TODO.md` and the two planner shape-spec documents, not from host-local copies.

2026-04-21: Lane 2 execution handoff now persists first-class `executionBriefs` on each idea with `briefId`, `sliceId`, revisioned provenance refs to design/plan/task-set state, and structured current execution-brief pointers while `currentBriefBySlice` remains a summary plus legacy-hydration field.

2026-04-21: `plan_refresh`, `task_add`, `task_done`, `task_remove`, and `task_reopen` now stale the current fresh execution brief; `task_done` and `task_reopen` require a regenerated fresh brief before further execution-state progress.

2026-04-21: Package docs, bundled planner/implementer skills, release notes README, and tests were synced to the persisted ExecutionBrief freshness model; local verification passed `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm test`, and `pnpm build` in `openclaw-workflow-planner`.

2026-04-21: Repo-local work on `openclaw-workflow-planner` now reflects a lane-2 persisted ExecutionBrief model: fresh brief gating controls `execution` phase entry, stale brief summaries leave control-plane phase in `planning`, and docs/tests were updated to match the container-canon execution-handoff semantics.

2026-04-21: Migration-hardening now surfaces derived control-plane `migrationState` for each request: `legacy_hydrated` for safe read-old normalization and `migration_required` with active blockers/unresolved reasons for ambiguous legacy brief summaries.

2026-04-21: Legacy planner read/write coverage now includes safe hydration, ambiguous legacy brief mismatch blocking, and load-old/save-new transition to canonical v4 state without sticky migration flags.
2026-04-21: Package README and bundled planner skill now mention operator-visible migration signaling for legacy `WORKFLOW_PLAN.md` files.
2026-04-21: `openclaw-workflow-planner` now exposes migration-hardening signals in derived control-plane runtime rather than silently normalizing all legacy planner files without operator visibility.

2026-04-21: Pointer-truth inside the migration boundary is stricter now: ambiguous legacy brief summaries no longer synthesize a resolved current execution-brief pointer for the current slice; they emit an unresolved pointer with operator-visible reason text.

2026-04-21: Execution-state gating now depends on pointer resolution truth as well as brief freshness; unresolved current execution-brief pointers block `task_done` and `task_reopen` with an explicit migration-oriented runtime error.

2026-04-21: Added regression coverage for unresolved current execution-brief pointers in ambiguous legacy files, and local verification passed `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm test`, and `pnpm build` in `openclaw-workflow-planner`.
2026-04-21: `openclaw-workflow-planner` migration hardening now includes pointer-truth semantics, so ambiguous legacy brief state cannot silently look execution-ready via synthetic resolved pointers.

2026-04-21: Slice 1b residue now persists minimal package-local artifact linkage for design, plan, task-set, and execution-brief records via logical `artifactRefs`, threads those refs through provenance `governingArtifactRefs`, and rebuilds derived control-plane artifact/entity linkage from the canonical planner state block.

2026-04-21: Pointer/provenance polish now preserves execution-brief creation provenance during stale transitions, clears orphaned current pointers on material design resets, and keeps ambiguous legacy brief state unresolved instead of silently promoting it.

2026-04-21: Added regression coverage for logical artifact refs/provenance shape plus a new `package-metadata.test.ts` harness for manifest/package posture; local verification passed `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm test`, and `pnpm build` in `openclaw-workflow-planner`.

2026-04-21: `openclaw-workflow-planner` now treats logical artifact refs as single-bundle provenance/materialization targets inside `WORKFLOW_PLAN.md`, not as separate persisted planner files, while still deriving artifact/entity linkage for control-plane inspection.

2026-04-21: Provenance/history metadata follow-up now normalizes partially populated provenance envelopes, carries idea-gate decision ids into design/plan/implementation brief provenance, and rebuilds revisioned entity/artifact history for design, plan, task-set, and execution-brief records.

2026-04-21: Current execution-brief pointer rebuild now prefers persisted artifact refs, degrades non-fresh resolved brief pointers to unresolved gating state, and repeated implementation_brief runs keep older executionBriefs as superseded persisted history while retargeting the current pointer.
2026-04-21: Package verification passed `pnpm check` in openclaw-workflow-planner after the provenance/history metadata and superseded-history regression slice landed.

2026-04-21: The latest planner-only slice closed provenance/history metadata coherence for `openclaw-workflow-planner`: superseded execution-brief history, pointer-truth on rebuild, provenance normalization, and planner-package docs/release-doc sync are aligned with package-level verification.
2026-04-21: Container TODO was reconciled with implementation reality so slices 1a through 6 are no longer tracked as fully open, and the active narrow tail is Slice 7 plus Slice 8 verification/docs.

2026-04-21: A narrow Slice 7 tail landed in `openclaw-workflow-planner`: current execution-brief invalidation is now centralized behind one helper, `plan_refresh` rename flows preserve `currentSliceId` while clearing stale title-keyed summary state, and new tests cover renamed `currentSlice` regeneration plus action-level stale-revision races for `implementation_brief` vs `task_done` and `task_add`.

2026-04-21: Package docs now state that `currentSliceId` is the durable slice identity and that overlapping action writes use the same fail-fast concurrency rule as direct file saves; `pnpm check` passed afterward in `openclaw-workflow-planner`.

2026-04-21: After reconciling container TODO against shipped code, the next-step posture for `openclaw-workflow-planner` moved from broad substrate refactor to a narrow Slice 7 tail; release-hardening was deferred until rename/concurrency proof landed.

2026-04-21: Slice 8 docs/verification tail is now closed in current implementation reality: package docs include an accepted-plan-to-close example, release docs mention the closure tail and verification posture, and `pnpm check` remained green.
2026-04-21: Container TODO was updated again so Slice 8 is no longer tracked as open after the end-to-end docs example and verification tail landed.
2026-04-21: After Slice 8 closure, the next intended lane for `openclaw-workflow-planner` is release/pre-release hardening rather than more planner runtime refactor.
2026-04-21: Release/pre-release hardening now includes the first tracked public release record `docs/releases/openclaw-workflow-planner/v0.1.0.md` plus a planner release index entry for `v0.1.0`.

2026-04-21: `scripts/release-prep.mjs` now defaults to GitHub-first release posture and can generate a first public release record for the current package version without forcing a version bump; a temp sandbox dry run confirmed current-version first-release generation.

2026-04-21: Planner release docs now include package-local `TEMPLATE.md` and `TEMPLATE_CLAWHUB.md` files so release note drafting and optional ClawHub worksheet drafting follow a package-owned template posture.

2026-04-21: `scripts/release-prep.mjs` now generates a companion `vX.Y.Z.clawhub.md` worksheet when `--publish-flow` requests a ClawHub path, with source repo, package name, display name, and source ref prefilled from package metadata; temp sandbox verification passed for both GitHub-first and ClawHub flows.

2026-04-21: Planner release prep now targets `0.1.1` with refreshed outcome-first package metadata/README copy for ClawHub positioning and a package-local `v0.1.1.clawhub.md` artifact that contains only the exact manual ClawHub release form fields.

2026-04-21: Release governance and `scripts/release-prep.mjs` now require marketing-description/changelog review in release prep, and ClawHub worksheet generation now emits exact manual UI fields instead of a broader operator worksheet; sandbox verification covered both manual archive-upload and CLI publish paths.
